vectors.description: Single deposit of 1000 units of asset A. No inputs, one output commitment.
vectors.name: deposit_only

vectors.description: Spend two notes of asset A (500 + 700 = 1200), produce two new notes (800 + 400). public_amount = 0.
vectors.name: transfer_2in_2out_same_asset

vectors.description: Merge two notes (250 + 750) into one 1000-unit note.
vectors.name: transfer_2in_1out_merge

vectors.description: Withdraw the entire balance of a 2500-unit note. No change note.
vectors.name: withdraw_full

vectors.description: Withdraw 500 of a 2000-unit note; keep a 1500-unit change note shielded.
vectors.name: partial_withdraw_with_change_note

vectors.description: Sum of inputs (100) + public_amount (0) ≠ sum of outputs (500). Circuit MUST reject.
vectors.name: invalid_value_conservation

vectors.description: Input note is asset A but output note is asset B. Circuit MUST reject.
vectors.name: invalid_asset_mismatch

vectors.description: Initial spend of a 400-unit note. Both the proof and the on-chain submission MUST succeed.
vectors.name: double_spend_same_nullifier__first

vectors.description: Same input note re-spent. The proof itself is still cryptographically valid, but the on-chain program MUST reject because the nullifier already exists.
vectors.name: double_spend_same_nullifier__replay

vectors.description: Witness references a Merkle root that is not in the on-chain ROOT_HISTORY_SIZE = 256 window. The on-chain program MUST reject.
vectors.name: root_not_in_history

vectors.description: Output amounts sum exceeds 2^64 - 1. The 64-bit range check inside the circuit MUST reject this witness.
vectors.name: amount_overflow

vectors.description: Proof is cryptographically valid and binds to ext_data_hash = ext_in_proof, but the on-chain transaction's recomputed ext_data_hash differs. Program MUST reject.
vectors.name: ext_data_binding_mismatch
